title: Bridge Essay: The Association between Lifelong Greenspace Exposure and 3-Dimensional Brain Mag

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

**The Association between Lifelong Greenspace Exposure and 3-Dimensional Brain Magnetic Resonance Imaging in Barcelona Schoolchildren.**. Environ Health Perspect.. PMID: 29504939. DOI: 10.1289/EHP1876. BACKGROUND: Proponents of the biophilia hypothesis believe that contact with nature, including green spaces, has acrucial role in brain development in children. Currently, however, we are not aware of evidence linking such exposure with potential effects on brain structure. OBJECTIVE: We determined whether lifelong exposure to residential surrounding greenness is associated with regional differences in brain volume based on 3-dimensional magnetic resonance imaging (3D MRI) among children attend

notes: Auto-generated from existing work JSON on 2026-07-12. Phase 15 (Ecology/Animism).
